channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tracking the hot-button issues driving this year's midterms politics
item.description: Record high oil prices, war with Iran, and controversial immigration policies are just some of the issues for voters. NPR's Domenico Montanaro talks about covering this year's complex midterms.
item.content:encoded: Record high oil prices, war with Iran, and controversial immigration policies are just some of the issues for voters. NPR's Domenico Montanaro talks about covering this year's complex midterms.
item.dc:creator: Elena Burnett

item.title: War with Iran has aviation costs sky high. Are there alternatives to jet fuel?
item.description: As the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz has sent jet fuel costs and airplane seat prices soaring. NPR's Emily Kwong looks at the viability of alternatives such as sustainable aviation fuel.
item.content:encoded: As the blockade of the Straight of Hormuz has sent jet fuel costs and airplane seat prices soaring. NPR's Emily Kwong looks at the viability of alternatives such as sustainable aviation fuel.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: What to know about the Canvas hack of student data
item.description: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.content:encoded: Millions of students in universities and K-12 districts had their data compromised this week as a hack took down Canvas, a classroom management tool used all over the country.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Mideast war worsens conflict between elephants and villagers in Sri Lanka
item.description: Conflict between farmers and elephants in Sri Lanka has grown worse as food and fuel are scarcer due to war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: Conflict between farmers and elephants in Sri Lanka has grown worse as food and fuel are scarcer due to war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Red Square V-Day parade scaled back due to Ukrainian drone strike fears
item.description: Foreign press was kept away from Russia's Victory Day Parade in Moscow amid fears of a Ukrainian strike.
item.content:encoded: Foreign press was kept away from Russia's Victory Day Parade in Moscow amid fears of a Ukrainian strike.
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: It was 'gut instinct,' say The Black Keys about new album, Peaches!
item.description: A conversation with eclectic rock duo, Patrick Carney and Dan Auerbach of the The Black Keys, about the unique motivations behind the making of their new album, Peaches!
item.content:encoded: A conversation with eclectic rock duo, Patrick Carney and Dan Auerbach of the The Black Keys, about the unique motivations behind the making of their new album, Peaches!
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: 'We're dry:' The new U.S. Wildland Fire Service prepares for extreme fire season
item.description: Brian Fennessy, new head of the U.S. Wildland Fire Service, says his agency is 'trying to bring on additional aircraft and bring them on early,' and dismisses criticism of prevention methods.
item.content:encoded:
Brian Fennessy, new head of the U.S. Wildland Fire Service, says his agency is 'trying to bring on additional aircraft and bring them on early,' and dismisses criticism of prevention methods.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Soccer was once considered niche in the U.S. Then came the 1994 World Cup
item.description: Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?
item.content:encoded:
Soccer — or football as it's known around the globe — was far from mainstream in the U.S. leading up to the 1994 World Cup. But in the end, the tournament was considered a resounding success. How exactly did that happen?

(Image credit: Lois Bernstein)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Lack of U.S. response to hantavirus outbreak worries public health experts
item.description: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.content:encoded: Public health experts are raising concerns about why the U.S. government hasn't had a more public response to the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Virginia Supreme Court rules against voters' referendum on redistricting
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.content:encoded: The Virginia Supreme Court has delivered a striking blow to Democrats in the redistricting battle they're waging against Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Jahd Khalil
